meaning: n.(国王的)侍从,(贵族的)管家,收款人
examples: Chamberlain scored 50 or more points four times in the season.
The tariff reform movement in Britain started by Joseph Chamberlain brought new opponents of Manchesterism.
Would you drive into Chamberlain with me?
Mark Chamberlain-Davidson was jailed for 18 months in 2006 for the attack at Castletown Harbour.
For now, he will not be joining seventh-inning specialist Joba Chamberlain on the 15-day disabled list.
